error_signature: empty result when filename contains brackets
title: Empty result when reading files with brackets in filename
symptom: When attempting to read a file with brackets in its filename, n8n returns an empty result. Removing the brackets allows the file to be read successfully.
root_cause: n8n's file reading mechanism may not handle filenames with brackets correctly, especially on Windows. This can lead to issues where the file cannot be found or read, resulting in an empty output.
fix_steps: Use double backslashes to escape brackets in the filename.
Ensure the path uses forward slashes instead of backslashes.
Use the .replaceAll() method to handle multiple occurrences of brackets.
agent_instruction: Escape brackets in the filename using double backslashes and ensure the path uses forward slashes.
